name: Dallas Dandy Brisket
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir together 2 tablespoons of the dry rub ingredients in a small bowl.
2. Combine 2 tablespoons of the rub with beer, chopped onion, cider or white vinegar, oil (corn or canola), and chipotle chiles plus adobo sauce in a blender and puree.
3. Place the brisket in a plastic bag and pour the marinade over it. Refrigerate the brisket overnight.
4. Before cooking, take the brisket from the refrigerator. Drain and discard the marinade. Pat the brisket down with all but 2 tablespoons of the remaining rub, coating the slab well.
5. Let the brisket sit at room temperature for about 45 minutes.
6. Prepare the smoker to bring the temperature up to 200 to 220 degrees F.
7. Transfer the brisket to the smoker and cook for 3 hours.
8. Place the meat on a sheet of heavy-duty foil, sprinkle with the rest of the rub, and close the foil tightly. Cook for an additional 1-1/2 to 2 hours until well-done and tender.
9. Let the brisket sit at room temperature for 15 minutes before trimming excess fat and slicing thinly against the grain.
Serve with your favorite barbecue sauce.
